sections.paragraph:
παροίχομαι pf. ptc. neut. παρῳχηκός (Tat. 26, 1) and παρῳχημένος mid. dep. to be past, of time (οἴχομαι ‘go, come’; Hom. et al.; Dionys. Hal. 11, 5 χρόνος; SIG 885, 5 διὰ τῶν παρῳχημένων χρόνων; CPR 10, 6; PRyl 153, 35; Jos., Ant. 8, 301) ἐν ταῖς παρῳχημέναις γενεαῖς Ac 14:16.—M-M.

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

dep. dep. = deponent

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

CPR CPR = Corpus Papyrorum Raineri—List 4

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 780
